channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Layoffs begin at US health agencies responsible for research, tracking disease and regulating food
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department began receiving notices of dismissal on Tuesday in an overhaul ultimately expected to lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida Republicans face off against well-funded Democrats in US House special elections
item.description: OCALA, Fla. (AP) — Tuesday's special elections for two Florida congressional seats in heavily pro-Trump districts have become an unexpected source of concern for national Republicans as Democrats have poured millions in fundraising into the races.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: GOP firebrand dares Dems to condemn attacks on Elon Musk's Tesla
item.description: Amid a spate of violent attacks against Tesla owners and dealers across the country, the GOP is challenging Democrats to condemn the attacks as domestic terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Intel Democrats call for damage assessment on Signal chat
item.description: Democrats on the House Intelligence Committee on Monday requested an independent assessment into Trump administration officials' use of Signal to discuss an airstrike, asking that Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard initiate such a process and delegate it to another official.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-backed bills targeting activist judges, noncitizen voting head for House-wide votes
item.description: Republicans are targeting the courts and noncitizen voting in a pair of bills advancing the Trump administration's agenda this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Booker talks through the night in marathon floor speech to protest Trump
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) pulled an all-nighter, delivering a marathon speech on the Senate floor that lasted well into Tuesday morning in a push to combat the Trump administration’s policies. Booker, a member of Democratic leadership, rose to speak around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump to focus on love as a 'universal language' at International Women of Courage Awards
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: First lady Melania Trump will recognize courageous women from all corners of the world at the State Department Tuesday and is expected to celebrate “the extraordinary strength of women who embody love in action around the globe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrat will block Trump’s VA nominees to protest cuts
item.description: Sen. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) says he will put a hold on President Trump’s nominees to lead the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to protest cuts at the agency that provides lifelong health care services to military veterans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Mass layoffs begin at the US Department of Health and Human Services
item.description: Employees across the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services began receiving notices of dismissal Tuesday, as part of a major overhaul expected to ultimately lay off up to 10,000 people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance doubles down after Trump admin admits 'error' sending man to Salvadoran prison
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said a man the Trump administration sent to a Salvadoran prison in error had "no legal right to be here" when questioned about the alleged mistake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's pick for Joint Chiefs chairman strikes a humble, apolitical tone in Senate hearing
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff acknowledges he's an unknown and unconventional nominee.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin withholds millions from Planned Parenthood for civil rights and executive order violations: report
item.description: The Trump administration has reportedly withheld millions of dollars from Planned Parenthood affiliates for violating civil rights laws and executive orders against DEI programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court candidates make final push amid high spending, voting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — President Donald Trump's preferred candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court and his Democratic-backed challenger made a final blitz across the state on Monday, the day before voting concludes in a race where early turnout has surged and spending is nearing $100 million.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he expects Musk to eventually return to running companies
item.description: President Trump on Monday said he expects Elon Musk will eventually return to running his companies after his work overhauling the federal government. A reporter noted that Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, is a special government employee, which means he can only work for 130 days in his role.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: US-Canada-Mexico joint World Cup goes from unity to acrimony thanks to tariffs and '51st state' talk
item.description: As the United States prepares to welcome the world for FIFA’s Club World Cup this June, golf’s Ryder Cup in September, the World Cup next year and the Los Angeles Olympics in 2028, the question becomes: Will the world want to come.
item.dc:creator: Howard Fendrich, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: EPA chief Zeldin says he is closing the agency's one-room museum, saving taxpayers $600,000 a year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin says he is closing a one-room museum at the agency's Washington headquarters, saving taxpayers $600,000 a year in operating costs.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal employees union sues over Trump move to block collective bargaining
item.description: A federal employee union has sued to block a Trump administration order that would strip bargaining rights at more than a dozen departments.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: AG Keith Ellison won't run for U.S. Senate, backs Flanagan for DFL nod
item.description: Attorney General Keith Ellison removes himself from Senate consideration by backing Lt. Gov. Peggy Flanagan for DFL nod.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida House proposes largest sales tax cut in state history
item.description: The Florida House of Representatives is proposing the largest cut to sales tax in state history, from 6% to 5.25%.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California lawmaker revives effort to end forced prison labor
item.description: A new California bill seeks to prohibit the Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation from forcing inmates to work.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
